---
title: "Experience"
toc: false
---

An overview of my professional trajectory in meteorology, climate services, and atmospheric research.

---

::: {.award-entry}
### Climate Services Technician
**Meteorological Service of Catalonia (SMC)** · Departament de Territori, Habitatge i Transició Ecològica  
📅 February 2024 – present · 📍 Barcelona

Providing climate data and products to describe recent weather evolution in its climatic context. Supporting decision-making processes and responding to meteorological information needs, under the Programme for Drought Management Support (*Programa de Suport a la Gestió de la Sequera*).
:::

::: {.award-entry}
### Research Engineer — Atmospheric Composition Group
**Barcelona Supercomputing Center (BSC-CNS)**  
📅 November 2023 – February 2024 · 📍 Barcelona

Collection and processing of emission data for use in modelling system development. Development and comparison of methodologies to estimate pollutant emissions. Modelling, post-processing and analysis of results (Python, R).
:::


::: {.award-entry}
### Air Quality Department Intern
**Meteosim SL**  
📅 October 2019 – March 2020 · 📍 Barcelona

Environmental consultancy support: GIS treatment of pollutant source locations and dispersion (QGIS). Meteorological simulations (WRF) and air quality simulations (AERMOD, CALPUFF). Development of emission inventories for the mining industry (QGIS, R).
:::

::: {.award-entry}
### Climatology Area Intern
**Meteorological Service of Catalonia (SMC)**  
📅 January 2018 – September 2019 · 📍 Barcelona

Support in meteorological reports and climate bulletins. Participation in climate data rescue, quality control and digitisation projects. Database exploitation (SQL), statistical analysis and visualisation of climate series (R).
:::

::: {.award-entry}
### Curricular Internship
**Institut Català de Ciències del Clima (IC3)**  
📅 July 2016 – September 2016 · 📍 Barcelona

Analysis of meteorological data from the ClimaDat network. Development of R scripts to identify and classify sea breeze days at the Ebro Delta. Support to a study on pollutant dispersion during sea breeze episodes.
:::
